_house_style: Content slides: 180-210 words (~90-120s at ~138 wpm). Title and closing slides: 30-60 words (exempt from the band; embed_notes and verify_deck exempt first/last by default). Short spoken sentences; no academic openers ('This slide marks...', 'At the heart of the module...'); end every slide with one strong line starting 'The takeaway: ...'. Keep citations on the slides, not spoken. No double-quotes inside values. Keys starting with _ are ignored by the scripts.
1: Title slide. Keep this one short, about 30 to 60 words. Say the module name, why it matters to a working researcher, and what they will be able to do by the end. Do not read the subtitle verbatim. The takeaway: this module turns AI from a novelty into a disciplined research instrument.
2: This is a full-length example slide showing the house style. Open with the contrast the slide draws, not an academic framing. Keep sentences short and spoken. Walk the audience through the left side first, then the right, then name the shift between them. Use plain language a professional would say out loud, not text they would read. Aim for roughly 180 to 210 words so the slide runs about 90 to 120 seconds at a natural pace. Avoid openers like 'This slide marks' or 'At the heart of the module'. Reference the on-slide evidence in passing but do not recite the citations aloud unless asked. Close with one strong line. The takeaway: structure beats cleverness, every time.
